exports.content: The momentum-resolved valence-band dispersion of single-crystalline orthorhombic CsPbBr$_3$ at room temperature was measured by angle-resolved photoelectron spectroscopy (ARPES); here the measured quasiparticle energy as a function of the in-plane crystal momentum was fitted near the valence-band maximum (VBM) by the isotropic in-plane parabolic dispersion E(k)=E_{\\mathrm{VBM}}+\\hbar^{2}k^{2}/(2m_{\\mathrm{exp}}), where k is the magnitude of the two-dimensional in-plane wave vector (units Å^{-1}), \\hbar is the reduced Planck constant, and m_e is the free electron mass, and the energy zero was set at the VBM defined as the energy of the maximum of the energy-distribution curve (EDC) at the band top; this fit, performed on the upper valence band along the surface high-symmetry direction labeled \\bar{\\Gamma}-\\bar{M}, returns an experimental hole effective mass m_{\\mathrm{exp}}=0.26\\pm0.02\\,m_e. [@Puppin2020]
exports.label: gcn_1544d55444d743c8
